fact: November 14 is exactly 318 days into the year, which means there are only 47 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many trees are completely naked - they've dropped almost all their leaves and stand like giant skeletons against the sky.
Polar bears in the Arctic are getting super chunky right now, packing on blubber before the sea ice forms so they can hunt seals all winter long.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're just getting home from school.
November 14 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when this mysterious water sign is at its most intense and secretive.
Deer are going absolutely bonkers right now - it's peak mating season, so male deer are fighting each other and making weird grunting noises to impress the ladies.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 14 is when jacaranda trees explode with bright purple flowers that carpet entire streets.
The Earth is tilted at just the right angle now that sunrise colors last way longer than in summer - the sky stays orange and pink for ages.
Woolly bear caterpillars are frantically searching for the perfect hiding spots to curl up and survive winter - some will freeze solid and thaw out alive in spring.
November 14 is roughly 37 days before the winter solstice, meaning we're racing toward the shortest day of the year at lightning speed.
Squirrels become total hoarders around this time, burying up to 10,000 nuts and somehow remembering where most of them are hidden.
The air pressure changes around mid-November create perfect conditions for those crisp, clear nights where stars look like glittering diamonds.
